title: Neural Nets Are Not Digital Brains
script_summary: Exploring how neural networks differ from biological brains reveals the truth behind AI’s capabilities.

segments.voiceover: Neural networks may seem like brains, but they’re just an imitation.
They borrow a metaphor, not a blueprint. Weights and layers mimic thought processes.
While your brain processes information through biological neurons, AI needs training data. That’s a huge difference.
In neural networks, each layer builds on the last, adjusting weights to refine predictions. But it’s not sentience.
Understanding this helps dismantle the AGI hype. Neural networks are powerful, but they're not conscious.
Curious about AI's true potential? Like and follow for more insights!
